bids.reasoning: At the opening with no opponent offers or messages yet, I am targeting my maximum utility bid. Since there is no time pressure at t=0.0, I should start firm on my preferred values across all issues and wait for the opponent’s response.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid while making a small concession to avoid simply repeating my opening offer. The opponent’s only offer was all valueA, which is the lowest utility for me on every issue and does not yet show meaningful concession, so I will hold firm especially on the high-weight issues D and E.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid around 0.98 while making only a small concession. The opponent has moved slightly from all valueA to valueB on issueD, but their offers are still very low for me, so I will hold firm on high-weight issues C/D/E and concede only on a low-weight issue to avoid repeating my prior bid.

It is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a small concession on my low-weight issues. The opponent’s offers have improved substantially for me, especially on issueD and issueE, but they are still weak on issueA/issueB/issueC; I will hold firm on the higher-weight issues and concede only on issueA and issueB for now.

At t=0.08 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.94 rather than making large concessions. The opponent’s offers have improved substantially for me on issueD and issueE, but they are still holding issueA, issueB, and issueC at my lowest values, so I will concede only on lower-weight issues while protecting the high-weight ones.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.92 because it is still very early at t=0.10, so there is little need for major concessions yet. The opponent has shown willingness to concede on high-weight issues D and E in some offers, while keeping A and B relatively low, so I am conceding mainly on low-weight issueA while holding firm on C/D/E.

I am targeting about 0.90 utility, since it is still early at t=0.12 and there is no need to concede heavily yet. The opponent’s bids have improved substantially for me, especially on high-weight issueD and issueE, so I will hold those at valueE while conceding mainly on low-weight issueA and partially on issueB. No opponent messages were provided, so I rely only on the offer history.

I am targeting about 0.90 utility, since it is still early at t=0.14 and the opponent has already shown willingness to move substantially on high-weight issueD and issueE. Their latest offers keep issueE at valueE but vary issueD and concede somewhat on lower-weight issues, so I am conceding mainly on my low-weight issueA and issueB while holding firm on issueC, issueD, and issueE.

I am targeting a strong utility around 0.86 while making a modest early concession on issueC and preserving my highest-weight issues, issueD and issueE, at valueE. The opponent’s offers have improved substantially for me and repeatedly include valueE on issueD/issueE, so I infer those may be acceptable; at t=0.16 there is still time to hold a favorable position while varying from my prior bid.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.88 because it is still early in the negotiation at t=0.18, so there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent has signaled willingness to give valueE on issueE and sometimes issueD, and their latest offer gave valueE on issueC, so I am holding firm on the high-weight issues while conceding slightly on low-weight issueB.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.86, conceding mainly on lower-weight issueA and issueB while holding the highly important issueD and issueE at valueE. At t=0.20 there is still time, but the opponent’s offers have improved substantially for me, with their latest offer reaching about 0.84 utility, so I am making a modest counter rather than dropping further. Their bidding suggests they may tolerate valueE on issueD/issueE and valueD on issueC, while still pushing hard on the lower issues.

I targeted a high-utility bid around 0.88 because it is still early at t=0.22, so I should not concede too much yet. The opponent’s offers have improved substantially for me, especially on issueD and issueE, so I am holding firm on those high-weight issues while conceding more on low-weight issueA and matching their recent issueB signal.

I am targeting about 0.88 utility, keeping the high-weight issues C, D, and E at valueE while making concessions on the lower-weight A and B. At t=0.24 there is still time, so I do not need to drop all the way to the opponent’s last 0.86-utility offer; their recent bids show willingness to give valueE on C/D/E and tolerate valueB on A or B. No opponent messages were provided, so I rely only on the offer history.

I am targeting a utility around 0.86: still above the opponent’s last offer to me, but with a meaningful concession on low-weight issueA and only moderate concessions on issueB/issueC. At t=0.26 there is no need to rush to accept, but the opponent’s recent bids have improved substantially and they have already shown willingness to give valueE on issueD/issueE, so I am holding firm mainly on those high-weight issues.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.88 because it is still early-mid negotiation at t=0.28, so there is no need to concede heavily yet. The opponent’s offers have improved substantially for me, especially on high-weight issueD and issueE, and their last offer already gives me valueD/valueE on the important issues, so I am holding firm there while making tradeoffs on lower-weight issueA and issueB.

I am targeting about 0.90 utility by holding firm on the high-weight issues C, D, and E while conceding mainly on the lower-weight issues A and B. At t=0.30 there is still enough time to avoid accepting the opponent’s 0.86-utility offer outright, but their recent bids show meaningful movement toward valueE on the important issues, so this counteroffer stays close to what they have signaled while improving my payoff slightly.

I am targeting a high but realistic utility around 0.88, since we are still only at t=0.32 and do not need to drop to the opponent's latest 0.82-utility bid yet. The opponent has conceded substantially on issueC and issueD and often keeps issueE high, though their latest offer moved issueE down to valueD, so I am holding firm on high-weight issueE while conceding low-weight issueA and issueB. There are no natural-language messages to weigh, so I rely only on the offer pattern.
